methods.description: The authors of ScaffoldAvatar present a hybrid pipeline that builds high-fidelity Gaussian head avatars by anchoring “patch expressions” -- localized Gaussian patches tied to a scaffold mesh -- to capture fine expression detail and enable robust animation.

methods.name: TeGA
methods.title: TeGA: Texture Space Gaussian Avatars for High-Resolution Dynamic Head Modeling
methods.description: The authors of TeGA introduce a high-detail 3D head avatar model that embeds 3D Gaussians within a continuous UVD texture space over a morphable head mesh -- allowing densification where detail matters while preserving efficient animation.

methods.name: GPS-Gaussian
methods.title: GPS-Gaussian: Generalizable Pixel-Wise 3D Gaussian Splatting for Real-Time Human Novel View Synthesis

continuations.name: GPS-Gaussian+
continuations.title: GPS-Gaussian+: Generalizable Pixel-Wise 3D Gaussian Splatting for Real-Time Human-Scene Rendering from Sparse Views
continuations.description: This follow‑up work extends GPS‑Gaussian by targeting human‑scene rendering.

methods.description: In this work, the authors propose a framework that generates 3D Gaussian representations from sparse input views using a learned regression of Gaussian parameters from 2D image planes. Beyond just human characters, it handles humans in the context of scenes, still under sparse‑view conditions, and renders them in real time.

methods.name: GauHuman
methods.title: GauHuman: Articulated Gaussian Splatting from Monocular Human Videos
methods.description: The authors of GauHuman present an avatar synthesis framework that uses 3DGS with LBS to animate full-body characters quickly. They encode Gaussians in canonical space and deform them via skinning to posed space, with modules refining pose and LBS weights for detail preservation.

methods.name: Gaussian Shell Maps
methods.title: Gaussian Shell Maps for Efficient 3D Human Generation
methods.description: The authors of Gaussian Shell Maps propose a volumetric representation that uses shell‑structured Gaussian distributions to represent the human body -- capturing geometry and appearance -- and enable fast 3D human synthesis and rendering.

methods.name: Mesh-Anchored Hash Blendshapes
methods.title: Efficient 3D Implicit Head Avatar with Mesh-Anchored Hash Table Blendshapes
methods.description: In this work, the authors propose a real‑time 3D head avatar system that uses a novel mesh‑anchored hash table blendshapes technique: multiple tiny hash tables are attached to vertices of a parametric face mesh and their embeddings are linearly blended (via weights predicted from a CNN) to represent expression‑dependent geometry and appearance. A lightweight MLP then predicts density and color from these embeddings for volumetric rendering, accelerated by a hierarchical kNN lookup.

methods.name: FlashAvatar
methods.title: FlashAvatar: High-Fidelity Head Avatar with Efficient Gaussian Embedding
methods.description: The authors propose FlashAvatar, a method for reconstructing a high-fidelity animatable head avatar from a short monocular video in minutes and rendering it at ~300 FPS on a consumer GPU. They embed a uniform 3DGS field on the surface of a parametric face model and learn additional spatial offsets for non-surface regions and subtle facial details.

methods.name: 3DGS-Avatar
methods.title: 3DGS-Avatar: Animatable Avatars via Deformable 3D Gaussian Splatting
methods.description: 3DGS-Avatar presents an animatable human avatar model using deformable 3D Gaussian splats. A canonical Gaussian field is combined with a pose-conditioned deformation network, improving generalization to unseen poses.

methods.name: GoMAvatar
methods.title: GoMAvatar: Efficient Animatable Human Modeling from Monocular Video Using Gaussians-on-Mesh
methods.description: GoMAvatar introduces the Gaussians-on-Mesh hybrid representation that attaches 3D Gaussian splats to a deformable mesh to get both high-quality appearance and efficient articulation. The model is trained end-to-end from a single monocular video.

methods.name: GAvatar
methods.title: GAvatar: Animatable 3D Gaussian Avatars with Implicit Mesh Learning
methods.description: The authors propose GAvatar, which builds animatable avatars using a 3DGS representation embedded in pose-driven primitives and further learns an SDF-based implicit mesh on top of the Gaussians to extract high-fidelity geometry and texture.

methods.name: MoRF
methods.title: MoRF: Mobile Realistic Fullbody Avatars from a Monocular Video
methods.description: In the paper, the authors propose MoRF that builds realistic full-body avatars from monocular video. It uses a mesh-based body proxy (SMPL-X), a neural texture, and per-frame warping fields to improve temporal consistency and appearance fidelity.

methods.name: POCA
methods.title: POCA: Post-Training Quantization with Temporal Alignment for Codec Avatars
methods.description: POCA studies quantization for avatar decoders, showing that naive quantization (8-bit and 6-bit) introduces temporal noise in animated avatars. POCA proposes a novel Post-Training Quantization scheme with temporal alignment that preserves visual fidelity while compressing the decoder by 5.3×.

methods.name: ReliaAvatar
methods.title: ReliaAvatar: A Robust Real-Time Avatar Animator with Integrated Motion Prediction
methods.description: The authors present ReliaAvatar, a real-time avatar animator that integrates full-body motion prediction into an autoregressive animation pipeline to handle low-quality or missing input signals.

methods.name: GGHead
methods.title: GGHead: Fast and Generalizable 3D Gaussian Heads
methods.description: The authors of GGHead propose embedding 3DGS within a 3D-GAN framework to learn a high‑fidelity, 3D‑consistent head prior from 2D image corpora. A CNN predicts Gaussian parameters over a template‑mesh UV layout. A novel total variation loss ensures geometric coherence, enabling real‑time rendering of full‑resolution heads without 2D super‑resolution.

methods.name: GEM (Gaussian Eigen Models)
methods.title: Gaussian Eigen Models for Human Heads
methods.description: The authors of GEM propose representing 3D head avatars using a linear eigen‑basis of 3D Gaussians - position, scale, rotation, opacity -- enabling a low‑dimensional, network‑free representation that is light, animatable, and real‑time friendly.

methods.name: Real-time High-Fidelity Avatars (Distributed MLPs)
methods.title: Real-Time High-Fidelity Gaussian Human Avatars with Position-Based Interpolation of Spatially Distributed Mlps
methods.description: The paper proposes a 3DGS-based avatar synthesis where multiple MLPs are spatially distributed across the body and each Gaussian’s properties are interpolated from nearby MLPs' outputs.

methods.name: FADA
methods.title: FADA: Fast Diffusion Avatar Synthesis with Mixed-Supervised Multi-Cfg Distillation
methods.description: The authors propose a mixed-supervised loss to address the problem of poor distilled diffusion model performance with open-set input images. They also propose a multi-CFG distillation with learnable tokens to utilize the correlation between audio and reference image conditions.

methods.name: GPAvatar (monocular)
methods.title: GPAvatar: High-Fidelity Head Avatars by Learning Efficient Gaussian Projections
methods.description: The authors of GPAvatar propose a method that reconstructs high-fidelity dynamic 3D head avatars from monocular videos using Gaussian splats in a high-dimensional embedding space.

methods.name: TaoAvatar
methods.title: TaoAvatar: Real-Time Lifelike Full-Body Talking Avatars for Augmented Reality via 3D Gaussian Splatting
methods.description: TaoAvatar presents a high-fidelity, lightweight pipeline for creating full-body talking avatars optimized for AR devices. The method implies binding 3D Gaussians to a clothed human parametric template and distilling pose-dependent non-rigid deformations into an MLP to develop proper blend-shapes.

methods.name: RGBAvatar
methods.title: RGBAvatar: Reduced Gaussian Blendshapes for Online Modeling of Head Avatars
methods.description: RGBAvatar proposes an online framework for animatable head avatar modeling using a reduced Gaussian blendshape representation. Instead of fixed 3DMM bases, a compact learned space is created for each individual, improving identity accuracy and expressiveness. A color initialization scheme and batch-parallel Gaussian rasterization enable real-time training and inference.

methods.name: MobilePortrait
methods.title: MobilePortrait: Real-Time One-Shot Neural Head Avatars on Mobile Devices
methods.description: MobilePortrait introduces a novel method for real-time head avatar synthesis on mobile devices. Lightweight U-Net backbones are used to reduce computational requirements. To compensate for possible quality loss, the authors mix explicit and implicit keypoints for motion modeling and utilize precomputed visual features for foreground and background synthesis.

methods.name: LHM
methods.title: LHM: Large Animatable Human Reconstruction Model for Single Image to 3D in Seconds
methods.description: LHM proposes a feedforward model that reconstructs detailed, animatable 3D humans from a single image in seconds -- representing geometry and appearance with 3D Gaussian splats. It uses a multimodal transformer to fuse image features, body positional priors, and a head feature pyramid encoding to preserve facial identity and fine detail.

methods.name: GraphAvatar
methods.title: GraphAvatar: Compact Head Avatars with Gnn-Generated 3D Gaussians
methods.description: GraphAvatar proposes to replace explicit storage of 3D Gaussians for head avatars with a compact GNN that generates Gaussian attributes from a tracked mesh.

methods.name: SqueezeMe
methods.title: SqueezeMe: Mobile-Ready Distillation of Gaussian Full-Body Avatars
methods.description: SqueezeMe shows how to distill high-fidelity 3D Gaussian full-body avatars into a lightweight representation suitable for mobile devices by compressing Gaussian decoding and reducing compute/memory overhead while preserving animation and rendering quality.

methods.name: LAM
methods.title: LAM: Large Avatar Model for One-Shot Animatable Gaussian Head
methods.description: The authors of LAM propose a method that builds a fully animatable 3D Gaussian‑head avatar from a single input image in a single forward pass. No video, no multi-view rig, no post‑processing are needed.

methods.name: HGC-Avatar
methods.title: HGC-Avatar: Hierarchical Gaussian Compression for Streamable Dynamic 3D Avatars
methods.description: The paper proposes a hierarchical compression scheme for dynamic Gaussian‑based avatars, aimed at efficient streaming and rendering. It splits the representation into a structural layer (pose‑to‑Gaussian generator) and a motion layer (via SMPL‑X), enabling compact transmission, progressive decoding, and controllable rendering under new poses.

methods.name: Lodge
methods.title: Lodge: A Coarse to Fine Diffusion Network for Long Dance Generation Guided by the Characteristic Dance Primitives
methods.description: The method generates long dance motion sequences by using a coarse-to-fine diffusion network guided by extracted dance primitives, allowing both global structure and fine motion details across time.

methods.name: Make-Your-Anchor
methods.title: Make-Your-Anchor: A Diffusion-Based 2D Avatar Generation Framework
methods.description: The authors address the problem of full-body avatar synthesis where movements are “anchored” to the ones from the video. Specifically, they propose a novel system, Make-Your-Anchor, that only needs a one-minute video for training to enable precise translation of torso and hands. A structure-guided diffusion model is fine-tuned to take 3D mesh conditions as a separate modality.

methods.name: Loopy
methods.title: Loopy: Taming Audio-Driven Portrait Avatar with Long-Term Motion Dependency
methods.description: In this work, the authors propose an end‑to‑end video diffusion model conditioned only on audio, designed to generate realistic portrait videos with natural long‑term motion. The model uses inter‑/intra‑clip temporal modules and an audio‑to‑latents mapping so it can leverage long‑range temporal dependencies and produce smooth, expressive motion from audio alone.

methods.name: Hallo
methods.title: Hallo: Hierarchical Audio-Driven Visual Synthesis for Portrait Image Animation

continuations.name: Hallo2
continuations.title: Hallo2: Long-Duration and High-Resolution Audio-Driven Portrait Image Animation
continuations.description: The follow-up paper presents Hallo2, a method that generates long (tens of minutes) and high-resolution (up to 4K) talking-head videos from a single reference image and input audio, maintaining temporal coherence and avoiding drift over time.

methods.description: Hallo proposes a diffusion-based framework for portrait image animation driven by audio. It provides a hierarchical audio-driven synthesis module that jointly generates lip motion, facial expressions, and head pose.

methods.name: DAWN
methods.title: DAWN: Dynamic Frame Avatar with Non-Autoregressive Diffusion Framework for Talking Head Video Generation
methods.description: DAWN presents a non-autoregressive diffusion‑based framework that generates full talking‑head videos (lip sync + head pose + blinks) from a single portrait and an audio clip.

methods.name: MimicMotion
methods.title: MimicMotion: High-Quality Human Motion Video Generation with Confidence-Aware Pose Guidance
methods.description: MimicMotion introduces a video generation framework that can produce long, high‑quality human motion videos guided by a pose sequence. The method relies on confidence‑aware pose guidance to weigh pose keypoints by reliability, regional loss amplification to preserve detail in important regions (e.g., hands), and a progressive latent fusion strategy to enable temporally coherent videos of arbitrary length.

methods.name: MaintaAvatar
methods.title: MaintaAvatar: A Maintainable Avatar Based on Neural Radiance Fields by Continual Learning
methods.description: MaintaAvatar tackles the problem of updating a 3D avatar over time as a person’s appearance or pose changes, without losing the ability to render previous appearances. The method augments a NeRF-based avatar with a Global-Local Joint Storage Module and a Pose‑Distillation Module.
